unit_24 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_24

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 474 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i)______ moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seen merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii)______. To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii)______ cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound
marginalidentifyaccessoryapt
marginal: standard unit headword | identify: standard unit headword | accessory: standard unit headword | apt: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　an honorific; established; disputed
malignhonorificestablish
malign: standard unit headword | honorific: standard unit headword | establish: standard unit headword
#3 SE
In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.
答案：A, F　unassuming; modest
deprecateself-deprecating

答案：A　heterogeneous
simplisticpredictable

Blank 2
答案：A, E　mishandled; arrest

答案：D, E　elemental; rudimentary

Thus, when we are considering a role for sleep in human memory consolidation, we are referring to a possible role in the (ii)______ encoding of information and optimizing of recall, not a (iii)______ of sleep for recalling events of the prior day.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　disgorge; longer-term; requirement

答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent

答案：C, F　unreserved; wholehearted

Blank 2
Blank 3

#53 SE

答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot

Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual processes. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

For example, mountain glaciers tend to be (iii)______: once established, they increase the reflectivity of the surface, thus lowering temperatures and allowing more ice to form.There are two opposing theories about mountain formation and climate over the past 40 million years: either the surge of mountain building (i)______ the global cooling, or vice versa. 答案：C, E, I　caused; feedback between; self-perpetuating

When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the establish view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the establish view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
establish
establish: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
Investors are grateful that the attorney general has stepped in to pursue inquiries into the misfeasance in the financial markets, given that the regulators officially charged with policing the industry have been ______.Investors are grateful that the attorney general has stepped in to pursue inquiries into the misfeasance in the financial markets, given that the regulators officially charged with policing the industry have been ______.
答案：B　diffident
straightforward
straightforward: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically ______ study and nothing more.Shifting Fortunes take a conventional approach to American union history by simply explaining the reasons behind union growth and decline in the nineteenth and twentieth centuries: it is a chronologically and thematically ______ study and nothing more.
答案：B, E　confined; circumscribed
decline
decline: standard unit headword
#106 TC DOUBLE
To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
predictable
predictable: standard unit headword
#107 TC TRIPLE
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
decline
decline: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly ______ group of thinkers.Politicians who invoke the founders of the United States in support of their views seem to imply that the founders consistently concurred in their own views when in reality they were a highly ______ group of thinkers.
答案：D　contentious
predictable
predictable: standard unit headword

Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called ______.Well organized and researched and including all significant discoveries of medical scientists, the History of Western Medicines has been justly called ______.
答案：A, C　encyclopedic; exhaustive
encyclopedic
encyclopedic: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.Conventionally, the ultimate measure of a scientific work’s validity is how broadly and confidently its conclusions become accepted in the relevant field, which in turn (i)______ the extent to which its findings are replicated and extended. However, establishing such validity, especially for a novel experimental finding, can take years, and what (ii)______ replication or extension may be (iii)______ for some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　derives from; constitutes; disputed
establish
establish: standard unit headword

As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) ___________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed unaggressive and unwarlike.Mead was certainly (i) ___________ to state the case for Arapesh nonviolence and gentleness in absolute terms, for the Mountain Arapesh had an established tradition of warfare. As a relative proposition, however, her position was more (ii) ___________: compared to other groups in New Guinea, the Mountain Arapesh might have seemed unaggressive and unwarlike.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　mistaken; defensible

答案：D, F　rudimentary; sketchy

答案：D　disingenuous

With a single track bisecting the state and a handful of spokes to the east and south, Alaska is notable for its (iii)______ of transportation options.

In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.In the mid-1990s, researchers were aware that individuals and subpopulations vary in their susceptibilities to environmental pollutants. However, environmental health scientists, hoping to identify general trends, tended to view these variations as (i) __________ and used methods, in both human and animal studies, to (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　distractions; minimize their effect

identify: standard unit headword

答案：B　omnipresent

Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the single purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii) —he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii) force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i) one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. But it’s (i) to (ii) all 2,600 described species of mosquito when it’s just 80 or so—3 percent that drink human blood. Among those 2,520 relatively (iii) kinds of mosquitoes, there’s even one we’d like to see in greater numbers: Taxorhynchites, the mosquito that eats other mosquitoes.There is no sense trying to rehabilitate the reputation of the mosquito; nobody loves such a creature. Recent research suggests that social complexity and social intelligence are widespread among monkeys and apes. The use and manufacture of tools, by contrast, is (i) __________ two of the great apes, chimpanzees and orangutans, and tool use among monkeys appears to be quite (ii) __________.Recent research suggests that social complexity and social intelligence are widespread among monkeys and apes. The use and manufacture of tools, by contrast, is (i) __________ two of the great apes, chimpanzees and orangutans, and tool use among monkeys appears to be quite (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　restricted almost entirely to; rudimentary
rudimentary
rudimentary: standard unit headword

After the Turkish Republic was established traditional hamars (bathhouses) seemed to many Turks to be outmoded, but thanks to tourism, hamers have experienced a ______, becoming important cultural sites for foreign and Turkish visitors alike.After the Turkish Republic was established traditional hamars (bathhouses) seemed to many Turks to be outmoded, but thanks to tourism, hamers have experienced a ______, becoming important cultural sites for foreign and Turkish visitors alike.
答案：D, F　revival; renaissance
establish
establish: standard unit headword

Predators try to (i) their ultimate energy intake by (ii) the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy—that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging. [@W(SARS: sksIMGRE)Predators try to (i) their ultimate energy intake by (ii) the effort they expend searching for and handling prey relative to the energy the prey provides. Thus, a particular kind of prey would be (iii) to a predator's diet only if it is worth the predator's time and energy—that is, if it increases the animal's overall energy profit during foraging. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　maximize; reducing; added

Nevertheless, because scientific knowledge of the dynamics of land ice sheets is (i) __________, there is a possibility that the risk of significant melting of land ice (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　rudimentary; has been underestimated

答案：E, F　transitory; temporary

It is crucial, however, to (ii) . In other words, one needs to resist (iii) generic “manners” with more linguistically and historically precise designations, no least because Austen herself distinguishes.One of the problems with recent books that perpetuate the view of Jane Austen as moral tutor, a sort of Miss Manners for the ages, is that they understand manners as (i) , aS near-universal and timeless behavioral ideals or, worse still, a set of rules to be followed. In other words, one needs to resist (iii) generic “manners” with more linguistically and historically precise designations, no least because Austen herself distinguishes.

The defining characteristic of English company law at that time was its extreme degree of permissiveness: laissez-faire ruled in Parliament, caveat emptor in the courts, creating the ideal breeding ground for fraud and dishonesty. Yet these were the years when London became the financial center of the world and established its status as a (iii) __________ place to do business.Nineteenth-century England presents something of (i) __________, at least to those who believe that (ii) __________ best promotes business ethics. It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.It has been demonstrated that on Earth, no single landscape feature can be used as a quantitative indicator of past climatic conditions. The same is probably true for Mars; nevertheless, as the martian landscape is not as (i) __________ as the terrain on Earth, with no plate tectonics, no vegetation, less water, and lower erosion rates, the landscape's (ii) __________ the climate on Mars could be (iii) __________.

Blank 2
Blank 3

Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) , as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) , as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. But in the 1990s a small British theater company, Shared Experience, began to (iii) __________ the received wisdom about adaptations by developing thrilling and uniquely theatrical versions of several classic novels.Famous books have been (i) __________ the theater: Dickens suffered so much hackneyed staging that he felt forced to change his writing style, throwing time shifts into his narratives to trip up the adapters. Blank 2

But it is also true that honest history arises from a sense of (ii) __________. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　weave; interruptedness; strangeness

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　in overall equilibrium; fluctuations

答案：A, D　blithe; winsome

Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.

Blank 3

Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　Regrettably; cultural exchange in; an anomaly

#295 TC TRIPLE
Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.Analysis of 47.5-million-year-old fossils from Pakistan has yielded fresh insights into the early ancestors of modern whales. For example, Maiacetus inuus was a land animal (i)______ life in the sea. One Mariacetus inuus fossil encased a fetus positioned for a head-first delivery, which is typical of a land mammal and suggests the species gave birth onshore. But it probably spent much of its time (ii)______: its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii)______ walking.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　adapted to; in the water; clumsy for

predator: standard unit headword
#296 SE
In principle, the idea of an ice age is __________: in the past, it was colder, glaciers were much more extensive than they are today, and huge ice sheets covered large sections of continents that are now ice-free.In principle, the idea of an ice age is __________: in the past, it was colder, glaciers were much more extensive than they are today, and huge ice sheets covered large sections of continents that are now ice-free.
答案：C, D　simple; straightforward

straightforward: standard unit headword
#297 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)______. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)______ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
straightforward
straightforward: standard unit headword
#298 SE

That view has changed: the placebo effect is today seen as an important part of the healing process.
答案：C, E　an impediment; annoyance

The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. But what these blunders had in common was that no law of physics, or at least no (iii) __________ one, was invoked to support the claim.This is not the first time science has been invoked to argue for inherent limits to the capabilities of technology. The most (i) __________ of such predictions made in the name of science, such as that we would never go to the Moon, have underestimated the (ii) __________ of engineers. Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　embarrassing; ingenuity; established

This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.

It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)______ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)______ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)______ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

答案：D, E　decorousness; sobriety

But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. This made it almost (ii) __________ to use them in the classroom and hard to use them for scholarly purposes, since many of these stories were only ever published in obscure journals that have been (iii) __________ for quite some time.For a variety of reasons that are still difficult to understand fully, Ralph Ellison's masterful short stories have never been (i) __________ until now. This made it almost (ii) __________ to use them in the classroom and hard to use them for scholarly purposes, since many of these stories were only ever published in obscure journals that have been (iii) __________ for quite some time.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　collected; impossible; defunct
honor
honor: standard unit headword

Boreal forest is at the southern boundary of the moss-dominated tundra, which remains characteristically treeless because its spongy surface retains water that cannot drain away through the underlying permafrost. 答案：B, E　facilitating; incursion

#433 TC TRIPLE
For Plato the art of music was so (i) moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seem merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii) . To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii) cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.For Plato the art of music was so (i) moral and political reality that any alteration to music system would necessarily require a corresponding political shift. But two and a half millennia later, when classical music is generally seem merely as a lifestyle accessory, Plato’s conception seems (ii) . To be sure, there are still people who consider classical music to be of (iii) cultural importance, but few of them are able to articulate this convincingly.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　firmly anchored in; absurd; profound

In the search for truth, the knowledge gained by scientists consists of approximations with varying degrees of certainty. Such (i) truth can be highly (ii) , as the rapid and relatively steady progress of medical knowledge well exemplifies.In the search for truth, the knowledge gained by scientists consists of approximations with varying degrees of certainty. Such (i) truth can be highly (ii) , as the rapid and relatively steady progress of medical knowledge well exemplifies.
答案：C, D　provisional; useful

#435 TC TRIPLE
The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)______. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)______ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)______ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforce

答案：B, D　moribund; vital

The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)______. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. Doing so may be (ii)______ in some kinds of terrain, such as Shetland where the Eurasian otters are active in daytime and have clear individual markings. There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)______.The research on otters' environmental requirements is surprisingly (i)______. One reason for this has to do with the estimation of how much they use different areas. Doing so may be (ii)______ in some kinds of terrain, such as Shetland where the Eurasian otters are active in daytime and have clear individual markings. There it is possible to identify the individuals over stretches of coast of a few kilometers and to see what kinds of coast they use. However, the field conditions are (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　difficult; relatively simple; exceptional

#445 TC TRIPLE
The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and to the extent practicable and permitted by law, and since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i) their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii) measures to (iii) risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and to the extent practicable and permitted by law, and since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i) their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii) measures to (iii) risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize

#449 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism

Blank 1

Blank 3
